seo.title: Building Research Agents with n8n AI Agents + Vex Guardrails
seo.description: Learn how to build research agents with n8n AI Agents + Vex guardrails. Detect failures, prevent drift, and ensure reliability.

content.intro: n8n AI Agents enable powerful Research Agents that perform multi-step web searches, data synthesis, and summarization through modular workflow nodes. These agentic workflows excel at gathering and analyzing information but are prone to issues like unvalidated outputs, recursive loops, and stale context injection. Vex provides essential guardrails to verify source accuracy, claim validity, recency, and bias before outputs reach end-users.

architectureOverview.heading: Research Agents Architecture with n8n AI Agents
architectureOverview.description:
Research Agents in n8n typically start with a trigger node (manual, webhook, or schedule) that feeds into an AI Agent node configured with research tools like web search, page scraping, and summarization. The AI Agent orchestrates tool calls through a chain of nodes: Search Agent → Analysis Agent → Synthesis Agent, with memory nodes maintaining conversation context across steps. Action nodes (email, database write, Slack) consume the final synthesized output.

Critical failure points occur between AI nodes where unvalidated tool outputs pass directly to downstream agents, and memory windows can inject outdated research context. Credential exposure happens when API keys leak through tool parameters in agent traces. Vex integrates as a verification webhook after each AI Agent node to catch these issues early.

The workflow follows a linear-but-recursive pattern: Trigger → AI Agent (Search) → Vex Verify → AI Agent (Analyze) → Vex Verify → AI Agent (Synthesize) → Vex Verify → Action.

risks.name: Workflow trigger chains causing recursive agent execution
risks.description: Research agents calling their own trigger webhooks create infinite loops during iterative web searches
risks.mitigation: Vex recursion detector blocks self-triggering workflows and enforces max iterations

risks.name: AI node outputs not validated before passing to action nodes
risks.description: Unverified search results with hallucinated sources propagate through analysis and synthesis steps
risks.mitigation: Vex source verification and claim accuracy checks after each AI node

risks.name: Credential exposure through agent tool call parameters
risks.description: Research tools logging API keys or session tokens in tool call traces
risks.mitigation: Vex PII redaction scrubs credentials from tool parameters before logging

risks.name: Memory window nodes injecting stale context into conversations
risks.description: Outdated research context from previous workflow runs poisons current analysis
risks.mitigation: Vex recency checks validate memory context timestamps against query time
